prompt:
STYLE LOCK: Period gothic live-action, mid-19th-century interiors; candlelight and deep shadows; desaturated cool-gray palette; naturalistic skin and fabric texture; no illustration or stylized animation

CHARACTER VARIABLES (use these identities consistently; do not redesign faces or wardrobe):
- Character_The_Narrator [The Narrator]: Lean man of middle years (about 40–50), pale sallow skin, hollow cheeks, dark disordered medium-length hair, bright intense dark eyes, thin tense mouth; plain dark wool waistcoat over white shirtsleeves, dark trousers; period clothing. Visual lock: Same lean pale face, dark disordered hair, and bright intense dark eyes in every scene; always plain dark waistcoat and shirtsleeves as default; never elderly, never white-haired, never the filmed blue eye. Voice: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

CAST COUNT: exactly 1 distinct on-screen character identity(ies) only — Character_The_Narrator. Do not invent extra people, duplicate faces, or crowd extras not listed.

AUDIO: REQUIRED native Grok off-camera voiceover. Character_The_Narrator narrates exactly: "The disease had sharpened my senses — not destroyed — not dulled them.". Start speaking immediately with "The" — do not skip, delay, or swallow the opening word. After the last word, hold a brief natural pause with a closed mouth (about half a second); do not freeze mid-syllable or trail into empty staring. Do not lip-sync on-screen cast to this VO. Secondary layer = soft room tone / Foley. VOICE LOCK Character_The_Narrator: Male, middle years; medium-high tense pitch; precise, controlled pace that sharpens into fevered urgency; intimate confessional energy, same voice on-camera and in V.O.

PREVIOUS CLIP (already provided as video input — continue from its last frame):
INT. BARE ROOM - NIGHT. The Narrator speaks. Character_The_Narrator ON CAMERA lip-syncs "but why will you say that I am mad?". Character_The_Narrator still wears plain dark waistcoat, white shirtsleeves, rolled cuffs Camera directive: Medium shot tightening, 35mm lens, camera eases closer on pale composed face as he questions madness. Performance: Acting intensity 7/10: Widening defensive eyes, nostrils flare, jaw set in challenge Optics: f/1.4 shallow depth of field, creamy soft bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219 film stock, desaturated cool-teal shadows and warm amber candle highlights

CONTINUITY: This is a seamless EXTENSION of the provided previous video. Pick up from its last frame. Same character identity, wardrobe, lighting, and location. Natural progressive motion only — do not invent a new establishing shot or redesign faces/outfits. IDENTITY: Match locked plate descriptions in CHARACTER VARIABLES exactly — do not drift to illustration, anime, cartoon, or a different face/wardrobe. On-screen: Character_The_Narrator.

THIS CLIP:
End cleanly when the spoken line and primary action finish — do not hold a frozen pose or empty silence after dialogue.
INT. BARE ROOM - NIGHT. The Narrator speaks. Character_The_Narrator ON CAMERA lip-syncs "The disease had sharpened my senses — not destroyed — not dulled them.". Character_The_Narrator still wears plain dark waistcoat, white shirtsleeves, rolled cuffs Camera directive: Close-up on Narrator's face, 50mm lens, static frame catching candle flicker across careful features. Performance: Acting intensity 5/10: Proud narrowed gaze, arched brow, calm smile never reaching eyes Optics: f/2.0 shallow depth of field, soft candlelit bokeh Color grading: Kodak Vision3 500T 5219 film stock, desaturated cool-teal shadows and warm amber candle highlights / 480p, 24fps

NEGATIVE: no legible text, no watermarks, no logos, no extra limbs, blur/obscure environmental signage or screens, no name tags, no name badges, no embroidered names, no lower thirds, no personal names on clothing or props,
